methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $5.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $2.5B - $10.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Crusoe Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $10B+

private.name: Ayar Labs
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $3.75B

private.name: Lambda
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series E), post-money $2.5B+

data.narrative:
SandboxAQ, an AI & Software company at Series E stage led by founder Jack Hidary, has raised over $950M and carries a current post-money valuation of $5.75B. The peer universe comprises 9 comparable companies—1 public (Palantir) and 8 private—including same-stage players Crusoe Energy ($10.0B), Ayar Labs ($3.8B), and Lambda ($2.5B).

Two valuation methods triangulate a defensible range. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier typical of Series E advancement, yielding $8.0B. Peer-median methodology across the three same-sector, same-stage comparables produces $3.8B, with a peer range of $2.5B to $10.0B, reflecting material dispersion in how the market prices Series E AI companies. The median peer valuation sits notably below SandboxAQ's current mark.

Synthesizing both methods yields a triangulated valuation range of $3.2B (low) to $9.3B (high), with a median of $5.9B. The current $5.75B valuation sits near the triangulated median and within the implied range, though the wide peer dispersion ($2.5B–$10.0B) reflects genuine uncertainty in comparable company selection and stage-specific risk pricing. Key uncertainties include the appropriate selection and weighting of peers in a nascent frontier-tech market and the relevance of public comps (Palantir) to early-stage private valuations.
